code:
export const DEFAULT_PAGES = [{
  id: 1,
  type: "cover",
  title: "The Magic Forest",
  subtitle: "A Story for Little Explorers",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #f9a825, #f57c00)",
  textColor: "#fff",
  fontSize: 36,
  image: "Images/ThankWordOne.png",
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageSize: "70%",
    imageShadow: "0 8px 28px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)",
    imageRadius: 16,
    gap: 16,
    padding: [28, 24],
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "subtitle", "facts", "emoji"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Magic Forests",
    facts: ["Forests cover about 31% of Earth's land surface.", "The Amazon Rainforest produces 20% of the world's oxygen.", "Some forests are called 'old-growth' because they are hundreds of years old.", "Many folklore traditions around the world feature enchanted or magical forests."],
    learnMore: "https://www.nationalgeographic.com/environment/article/rainforests"
  }
}, {
  id: 2,
  type: "content",
  title: "Once upon a time…",
  text: "Deep inside a sparkling forest, a tiny rabbit named Luna discovered something truly wonderful — a glowing golden acorn that hummed like a song.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #e8f5e9, #c8e6c9)",
  textColor: "#2e7d32",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1099/1099878.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageSize: 150,
    imageRadius: 50,
    // circular image
    imageBorder: "4px solid #a5d6a7",
    imageShadow: "0 4px 16px rgba(46,125,50,0.3)",
    gap: 14,
    padding: [28, 22],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.40)",
    cardRadius: 20,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(165,214,167,0.6)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Rabbits",
    facts: ["Rabbits are lagomorphs, not rodents — they have a second pair of incisor teeth.", "A female rabbit is called a doe; a male is a buck; a baby is a kitten or kit.", "Rabbits can see almost 360° around them without turning their heads.", "Acorns are seeds of oak trees and are an important food for many forest animals."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rabbit"
  }
}, {
  id: 3,
  type: "content",
  title: "The Wise Old Owl",
  text: "Luna ran to find her friend Oliver the Owl, who lived in the tallest oak tree. 'What does it mean?' she asked, holding up the shiny acorn.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #e3f2fd, #bbdefb)",
  textColor: "#1565c0",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/2922/2922562.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Quicksand', sans-serif",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "left",
    textAlign: "left",
    imageSize: 130,
    imageRadius: 12,
    imageBorder: "3px dashed #90caf9",
    imageShadow: "0 6px 20px rgba(21,101,192,0.25)",
    gap: 12,
    padding: [24, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.35)",
    cardRadius: 14,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.5)",
    factsBg: "rgba(227,242,253,0.55)",
    factsRadius: 12,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.4)",
    overlayCircles: false,
    // clean look for this page
    contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Owls",
    facts: ["There are over 200 species of owls found on every continent except Antarctica.", "Owls can rotate their heads up to 270° — but NOT all the way around.", "A group of owls is called a parliament.", "Owls have been symbols of wisdom in cultures from ancient Greece to Native America.", "Oak trees can live for over 1,000 years and a single tree can produce 20,000 acorns per year."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Owl"
  }
}, {
  id: 4,
  type: "content",
  title: "The Map Appears!",
  text: "Oliver smiled wisely. 'That acorn is a key!' Suddenly a magical map unfolded from inside the acorn — showing a path to the Rainbow Waterfall.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fce4ec, #f8bbd0)",
  textColor: "#880e4f",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1436/1436820.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Courgette', cursive",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    titleItalic: false,
    imageSize: 160,
    imageRadius: 8,
    imageBorder: "4px solid #f48fb1",
    imageShadow: "0 6px 24px rgba(136,14,79,0.22)",
    imageFit: "contain",
    gap: 16,
    padding: [26, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.32)",
    cardRadius: 22,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(244,143,177,0.5)",
    factsBg: "rgba(252,228,236,0.55)",
    factsRadius: 16,
    pageBorder: "none",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Maps & Cartography",
    facts: ["The oldest known map is the Babylonian Map of the World, carved on clay around 600 BCE.", "Cartography is the science and art of making maps.", "Before GPS, explorers used stars, compasses, and hand-drawn charts to navigate.", "Treasure maps with an 'X marks the spot' are mostly a Hollywood invention — real pirates used coded journals."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cartography"
  }
}, {
  id: 5,
  type: "content",
  title: "The Journey Begins",
  text: "Luna and Oliver packed a tiny backpack with berries and honey. Together they hopped and flew through the shimmering trees, following the golden path.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fff8e1, #ffecb3)",
  textColor: "#e65100",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/3106/3106776.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Pacifico', cursive",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    textFontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageSize: 145,
    imageRadius: 0,
    imageShadow: "0 8px 20px rgba(230,81,0,0.20)",
    imageBorder: "none",
    gap: 14,
    padding: [24, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.42)",
    cardRadius: 24,
    cardBorder: "2px solid rgba(255,224,130,0.7)",
    factsBg: "rgba(255,248,225,0.60)",
    factsRadius: 14,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,202,40,0.5)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Forest Berries & Honey",
    facts: ["Wild berries like blueberries, raspberries, and elderberries grow in temperate forests.", "Honey is made by bees from flower nectar — a single bee produces only 1/12 teaspoon of honey in its lifetime.", "Bears, birds, foxes, and many insects rely on berries as a major food source.", "Foraging (finding food in the wild) is one of humanity's oldest survival skills."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foraging"
  }
}, {
  id: 6,
  type: "content",
  title: "Rainbow Waterfall",
  text: "At last they found it! The Rainbow Waterfall sang seven colors into the sky. Luna placed the acorn at its base — and flowers bloomed everywhere! 🌸🌼🌺",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #ede7f6, #d1c4e9)",
  textColor: "#4527a0",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1931/1931792.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageSize: 170,
    imageRadius: 50,
    // circular frame
    imageBorder: "5px solid #ce93d8",
    imageShadow: "0 0 30px rgba(206,147,216,0.6), 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",
    gap: 14,
    padding: [24, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.30)",
    cardRadius: 20,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(206,147,216,0.5)",
    factsBg: "rgba(237,231,246,0.55)",
    factsRadius: 16,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(186,104,200,0.3)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Rainbows & Waterfalls",
    facts: ["Rainbows form when sunlight refracts (bends) inside water droplets and separates into its spectrum of colors.", "The seven colors of a rainbow are: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet.", "Angel Falls in Venezuela is the world's tallest waterfall at 979 metres (3,212 ft).", "Waterfalls near mist can produce 'moonbows' — rainbows lit by moonlight — at night.", "Flowers bloom because of a combination of light, warmth, water, and pollinator cues."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rainbow"
  }
}, {
  id: 7,
  type: "end",
  title: "The End",
  subtitle: "You are a wonderful reader! ⭐",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #1a237e, #0d47a1)",
  textColor: "#fff",
  fontSize: 32,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/2961/2961935.png",
  emoji: "💫",
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageSize: 190,
    imageRadius: 50,
    imageBorder: "4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4)",
    imageShadow: "0 0 40px rgba(255,255,255,0.25), 0 6px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)",
    gap: 18,
    padding: [32, 24],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.12)",
    cardRadius: 20,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
    factsBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.10)",
    factsRadius: 14,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "subtitle", "facts", "emoji"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "The Moon & Stars",
    facts: ["The Moon is about 384,400 km from Earth and takes ~27 days to orbit our planet.", "There are an estimated 200–400 billion stars in our Milky Way galaxy.", "The Moon's gravity is responsible for Earth's ocean tides.", "Reading stories before bed helps children develop vocabulary and imagination."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moon"
  }
}];

sourcesContent:
export const DEFAULT_PAGES = [
  {
    id: 1,
    type: "cover",
    title: "The Magic Forest",
    subtitle: "A Story for Little Explorers",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #f9a825, #f57c00)",
    textColor: "#fff",
    fontSize: 36,
    image: "Images/ThankWordOne.png",
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageSize: "70%",
      imageShadow: "0 8px 28px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)",
      imageRadius: 16,
      gap: 16,
      padding: [28, 24],
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "subtitle", "facts", "emoji"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Magic Forests",
      facts: [
        "Forests cover about 31% of Earth's land surface.",
        "The Amazon Rainforest produces 20% of the world's oxygen.",
        "Some forests are called 'old-growth' because they are hundreds of years old.",
        "Many folklore traditions around the world feature enchanted or magical forests.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://www.nationalgeographic.com/environment/article/rainforests",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 2,
    type: "content",
    title: "Once upon a time…",
    text: "Deep inside a sparkling forest, a tiny rabbit named Luna discovered something truly wonderful — a glowing golden acorn that hummed like a song.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #e8f5e9, #c8e6c9)",
    textColor: "#2e7d32",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1099/1099878.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageSize: 150,
      imageRadius: 50,            // circular image
      imageBorder: "4px solid #a5d6a7",
      imageShadow: "0 4px 16px rgba(46,125,50,0.3)",
      gap: 14,
      padding: [28, 22],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.40)",
      cardRadius: 20,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(165,214,167,0.6)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Rabbits",
      facts: [
        "Rabbits are lagomorphs, not rodents — they have a second pair of incisor teeth.",
        "A female rabbit is called a doe; a male is a buck; a baby is a kitten or kit.",
        "Rabbits can see almost 360° around them without turning their heads.",
        "Acorns are seeds of oak trees and are an important food for many forest animals.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rabbit",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 3,
    type: "content",
    title: "The Wise Old Owl",
    text: "Luna ran to find her friend Oliver the Owl, who lived in the tallest oak tree. 'What does it mean?' she asked, holding up the shiny acorn.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #e3f2fd, #bbdefb)",
    textColor: "#1565c0",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/2922/2922562.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Quicksand', sans-serif",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "left",
      textAlign: "left",
      imageSize: 130,
      imageRadius: 12,
      imageBorder: "3px dashed #90caf9",
      imageShadow: "0 6px 20px rgba(21,101,192,0.25)",
      gap: 12,
      padding: [24, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.35)",
      cardRadius: 14,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.5)",
      factsBg: "rgba(227,242,253,0.55)",
      factsRadius: 12,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.4)",
      overlayCircles: false,        // clean look for this page
      contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Owls",
      facts: [
        "There are over 200 species of owls found on every continent except Antarctica.",
        "Owls can rotate their heads up to 270° — but NOT all the way around.",
        "A group of owls is called a parliament.",
        "Owls have been symbols of wisdom in cultures from ancient Greece to Native America.",
        "Oak trees can live for over 1,000 years and a single tree can produce 20,000 acorns per year.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Owl",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 4,
    type: "content",
    title: "The Map Appears!",
    text: "Oliver smiled wisely. 'That acorn is a key!' Suddenly a magical map unfolded from inside the acorn — showing a path to the Rainbow Waterfall.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fce4ec, #f8bbd0)",
    textColor: "#880e4f",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1436/1436820.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Courgette', cursive",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      titleItalic: false,
      imageSize: 160,
      imageRadius: 8,
      imageBorder: "4px solid #f48fb1",
      imageShadow: "0 6px 24px rgba(136,14,79,0.22)",
      imageFit: "contain",
      gap: 16,
      padding: [26, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.32)",
      cardRadius: 22,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(244,143,177,0.5)",
      factsBg: "rgba(252,228,236,0.55)",
      factsRadius: 16,
      pageBorder: "none",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Maps & Cartography",
      facts: [
        "The oldest known map is the Babylonian Map of the World, carved on clay around 600 BCE.",
        "Cartography is the science and art of making maps.",
        "Before GPS, explorers used stars, compasses, and hand-drawn charts to navigate.",
        "Treasure maps with an 'X marks the spot' are mostly a Hollywood invention — real pirates used coded journals.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cartography",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 5,
    type: "content",
    title: "The Journey Begins",
    text: "Luna and Oliver packed a tiny backpack with berries and honey. Together they hopped and flew through the shimmering trees, following the golden path.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fff8e1, #ffecb3)",
    textColor: "#e65100",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/3106/3106776.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Pacifico', cursive",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      textFontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageSize: 145,
      imageRadius: 0,
      imageShadow: "0 8px 20px rgba(230,81,0,0.20)",
      imageBorder: "none",
      gap: 14,
      padding: [24, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.42)",
      cardRadius: 24,
      cardBorder: "2px solid rgba(255,224,130,0.7)",
      factsBg: "rgba(255,248,225,0.60)",
      factsRadius: 14,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,202,40,0.5)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Forest Berries & Honey",
      facts: [
        "Wild berries like blueberries, raspberries, and elderberries grow in temperate forests.",
        "Honey is made by bees from flower nectar — a single bee produces only 1/12 teaspoon of honey in its lifetime.",
        "Bears, birds, foxes, and many insects rely on berries as a major food source.",
        "Foraging (finding food in the wild) is one of humanity's oldest survival skills.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foraging",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 6,
    type: "content",
    title: "Rainbow Waterfall",
    text: "At last they found it! The Rainbow Waterfall sang seven colors into the sky. Luna placed the acorn at its base — and flowers bloomed everywhere! 🌸🌼🌺",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #ede7f6, #d1c4e9)",
    textColor: "#4527a0",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1931/1931792.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageSize: 170,
      imageRadius: 50,            // circular frame
      imageBorder: "5px solid #ce93d8",
      imageShadow: "0 0 30px rgba(206,147,216,0.6), 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",
      gap: 14,
      padding: [24, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.30)",
      cardRadius: 20,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(206,147,216,0.5)",
      factsBg: "rgba(237,231,246,0.55)",
      factsRadius: 16,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(186,104,200,0.3)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Rainbows & Waterfalls",
      facts: [
        "Rainbows form when sunlight refracts (bends) inside water droplets and separates into its spectrum of colors.",
        "The seven colors of a rainbow are: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet.",
        "Angel Falls in Venezuela is the world's tallest waterfall at 979 metres (3,212 ft).",
        "Waterfalls near mist can produce 'moonbows' — rainbows lit by moonlight — at night.",
        "Flowers bloom because of a combination of light, warmth, water, and pollinator cues.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rainbow",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 7,
    type: "end",
    title: "The End",
    subtitle: "You are a wonderful reader! ⭐",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #1a237e, #0d47a1)",
    textColor: "#fff",
    fontSize: 32,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/2961/2961935.png",
    emoji: "💫",
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageSize: 190,
      imageRadius: 50,
      imageBorder: "4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4)",
      imageShadow: "0 0 40px rgba(255,255,255,0.25), 0 6px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)",
      gap: 18,
      padding: [32, 24],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.12)",
      cardRadius: 20,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
      factsBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.10)",
      factsRadius: 14,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "subtitle", "facts", "emoji"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "The Moon & Stars",
      facts: [
        "The Moon is about 384,400 km from Earth and takes ~27 days to orbit our planet.",
        "There are an estimated 200–400 billion stars in our Milky Way galaxy.",
        "The Moon's gravity is responsible for Earth's ocean tides.",
        "Reading stories before bed helps children develop vocabulary and imagination.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moon",
    },
  },
];
